category_title: Struggling readers who use collaborative talk to clarify character motives and adapt schema
minimal_context: The student is participating in a literature discussion group about the novel 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life' to analyze character motives.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's look at your booklet and discuss the chapter in 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life' where the mailman delivers the box without an adult's signature.

STUDENT: Did the mom really want Jeremy to have the box?

TUTOR: What do you think about her motives?

STUDENT: Yeah, but Oswald said that, um, that mail dude was following along with it because he…

TUTOR: Keep reading the text to see if you can figure it out.

STUDENT: (Skims the pictures in the text) I'm confused by it. I get to the point that I barely understand it. I have a lot of stuff in my head that I don't know what it means.

options.A: Purposefully select novels and other texts written by and/or featuring people of color for the literature discussion groups.
options.B: Articulate the rules to all participants at the outset of the literature discussion groups and maintain them as the facilitator.
options.C: Create heterogeneous student groups based on reading EOG scores, individual education plans (i.e., modifications, learning disabilities, etc.), and teacher-observed social behaviors.
options.D: Establish a norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for evidence-based exchange of viewpoints during the literature discussion groups.
